sections.paragraph: m. properly separated [as if peeled off], used of a little flock separated from others. 1Ki 20:27, שְׁנֵי חֲשִׂפֵי עִוִּים LXX. δύο ποίμνια αἰγῶν. Vulg. duo parvi greges caprarum. Abulwalid MS. gives it well قطيعان, an Arabic word which corresponds both in etymology and signification. [“But perhaps it may be from the idea of driving a flock; compare حسف to drive a flock.” This word is only found defectively חָשִׂף.]
